clients.name: DIAMANDIA
clients.industry: Anti-inflammatory wellness and body sculpting tools — premium self-care and holistic body care
clients.products: DIAMANDIA sells a curated range of body and face wellness tools, bundles, digital products, and coaching experiences. Core products include body gua sha tools (Bian Stone, Siren, LALA, Muse — $75–$99), body brushes, castor oil, and bundled kits ($105–$275). Higher-ticket offerings include an 8-week Anti-Inflammatory Group Coaching Experience ($500) and an in-person Anti-Inflammatory Reset Retreat ($4,000). Digital products include seasonal recipe guides ($20). Price range spans $10–$4,000.
clients.audience: Women aged approximately 25–45 interested in holistic wellness, anti-inflammatory lifestyles, body sculpting, and non-toxic self-care. Likely health-conscious, wellness-educated consumers who are interested in reducing bloating, cellulite, inflammation, and puffiness through at-home rituals. Also appeals to brides-to-be (Bridal Glow collection) and those seeking alternatives to clinical or invasive beauty treatments. Buyers are willing to invest in premium self-care and may already follow clean beauty, functional wellness, or anti-inflammatory diet content.
clients.voice: DIAMANDIA writes with confident, empowering, and slightly aspirational energy — copy feels self-assured without being preachy ('Wear it unapologetically,' 'real body results'). The tone is results-forward and direct, leading with outcomes and transformation rather than ingredients or process. There is an underlying sensuality and goddess-coded femininity woven throughout naming conventions (Siren, LALA, Muse, Goddess Platinum) and product language.
clients.contentPillars: At-home gua sha tutorials and technique demos, Anti-inflammatory lifestyle tips and body care routines, Before/after body sculpting and depuffing results, Bundle and product education (what each tool does and why), Clean self-care and non-toxic product storytelling
clients.proofPoints: Products described as larger than standard gua sha tools (a specific functional differentiator). Bian stone material called out by name as a premium material. 'Made in USA' on apparel line. Castor oil is non-toxic, phthalate-free, cruelty-free, and vegan. Siren Gua Sha recommended to be kept in freezer for maximum depuffing — a specific use-case tip implying tested, practiced methodology. Retreat limited to 5 people, signaling exclusivity.
clients.cta: Click on the orange shopping cart below, tap to learn more
clients.avoidTopics: Medical or clinical weight-loss claims, aggressive or fear-based body language (the brand leans empowerment, not insecurity-based marketing), cheap or drugstore beauty comparisons, fast-fashion or disposable wellness aesthetics, anything that undercuts the premium and ritual-driven positioning
clients.extraContext: DIAMANDIA appears to be founder-led based on the personal coaching and retreat offerings — TikTok content likely performs best when it feels personal, embodied, and expert-led rather than corporate. The brand bridges physical products with a lifestyle philosophy centered on anti-inflammation, which is a strong TikTok content hook with existing search demand. Gua sha is a visually demonstrable tool — tutorial and GRWM-style content is a natural fit. The Bridal Glow collection signals a gifting and occasion angle worth activating. Bundle naming (Goddess Platinum, Siren, Muse) creates a mythology-coded brand world that can be leaned into for aspirational content. The $4,000 retreat and $500 coaching program suggest the founder has authority positioning worth building on TikTok as a credibility pillar — even if those products are not the primary conversion driver.

clients.name: Approved Science
clients.industry: Supplements — 10-year-old brand, Amazon-first, expanding to TikTok Shop
clients.products: Supplements including collagen, Shilajit, appetite suppressants, lift/firming cream. Lead SKU TBD — appetite suppressant has compliance considerations, Shilajit crowded. 100+ samples/month budget. Healthy margins.
clients.audience: Health-conscious consumers, primarily Amazon buyers. Budget: $5k–$10k/mo for creator retainers. Decision makers: Dan and Lenea at Marketily.
clients.voice: Science-backed, credibility-forward. Lab coat energy. 10 years of Amazon proof points.
clients.contentPillars: TBD — strategy call to define lead SKU and content angles
clients.proofPoints: 10 years in market, strong Amazon presence and reviews, proven product-market fit
